author: Claude Dariot
title: Zodiac and Sign Division
text: De Zodiaci & signorum divisione CAP. 1. ZODIACUS est circulus ex magnis in caelo, dividens sphaeram in duas partes aequales, cuius latitudo est duodecim graduum. Longitudo vero graduum trecentorum & sexaginta, ut mox dicetur apertius. Dividitur autem hic circulus in partes duodecim, quae signa vocantur, & nomina a proprietate quadam aut stellarum dispositione talia acceperunt. Aries, Taurus, Gemini, Cancer, Leo, Virgo, Libra, Scorpius, Sagittarius, Capricornus, Aquarius, Pisces. Quae signa quibusdam characteribus notantur. Characteres autem in hunc figurantur modum. Aries ♈, Taurus ♉, Gemini ♊, Cancer ♋, Leo ♌, Virgo ♍, Libra ♎, Scorpius ♏, Sagittarius ♐, Capricornus ♑, Aquarius ♒, Pisces ♓.
translation: Of the Division of the Zodiac and Signs. CHAPTER 1. The Zodiac is one of the great circles in the sky, dividing the sphere into two equal parts, whose latitude is twelve degrees. Its longitude, however, is three hundred and sixty degrees, as will be explained more clearly soon. This circle is divided into twelve parts, which are called signs, and they received such names from a certain property or disposition of the stars. Aries, Taurus, Gemini, Cancer, Leo, Virgo, Libra, Scorpio, Sagittarius, Capricorn, Aquarius, Pisces. These signs are denoted by certain characters. The characters are figured in this manner: Aries ♈, Taurus ♉, Gemini ♊, Cancer ♋, Leo ♌, Virgo ♍, Libra ♎, Scorpio ♏, Sagittarius ♐, Capricorn ♑, Aquarius ♒, Pisces ♓.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Degrees, Minutes, Seconds
text: Unumquodque rursus illorum signorum dividitur in triginta partes, quae gradus vocantur. Hacque ratione totus circulus 360 gradus in longitudine continet. Gradus item in sexaginta minuta sequitur. Minutum rursus in sexaginta secunda. Secundum in
translation: Each of those signs, in turn, is divided into thirty parts, which are called degrees. And by this reckoning, the entire circle contains 360 degrees in longitude. A degree, likewise, is followed by sixty minutes. A minute, in turn, by sixty seconds. A second by

author: Claude Dariot
title: Subdivisions of Degrees
text: sexaginta tertia, Tertium in sexaginta quarta partitur, ac sic per sexagenariam multiplicationem procedendo in infinitum usque.
translation: sixty thirds, a third by sixty fourths, and so on, proceeding by sexagesimal multiplication to infinity.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Planetary Motions and Natures
text: De planetis, eorum motibus & natura. CAP. II. SEPTEM sub Zodiaco sidera errantia sive planetae proprio & continuo motu moventur, diversisque temporum spatiis ipsum metiuntur: ut Saturnus in triginta annis: Iupiter in duodecim: Mars in duobus: Sol in 365. diebus, & propemodum sex horis: Venus & Mercurius similiter: Luna vero in vigintiseptem diebus, & fere octo horis. Secundum autem illorum planetarum & signorum qualitates, diversos motus, & varios adinvicem aspectus, animi tempora mutantur, variasque in haec inferiora effectiones habent. Quaequidem ut dignosci valeant, planetarum omnium naturam nosse oportet, quam statim dicere conabor.
translation: Of the planets, their motions and nature. CHAPTER II. Seven wandering stars or planets move under the Zodiac with their own continuous motion, and measure it in different periods of time: such as Saturn in thirty years; Jupiter in twelve; Mars in two; the Sun in 365 days and almost six hours; Venus and Mercury similarly; the Moon, however, in twenty-seven days and almost eight hours. According to the qualities of these planets and signs, their diverse motions, and various aspects to one another, the times of the soul change, and they have various effects on these lower things. Indeed, so that these may be known, it is necessary to know the nature of all the planets, which I will now attempt to describe.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Northern and Southern Signs
text: Signorum rursus multiplex est divisio. Primo enim in septentrionalia, & meridionalia signa dividuntur: quae sic dicuntur, quoniam ab aequatorem versus eam caeli partem vergunt. Septentrionalia ♈, ♉, ♊, ♋, ♌, ♍. Meridionalia ♎, ♏, ♐, ♑, ♒, ♓.
translation: Again, there is a manifold division of the signs. First, they are divided into northern and southern signs: which are so called because they incline towards that part of the sky from the equator. Northern: Aries, Taurus, Gemini, Cancer, Leo, Virgo. Southern: Libra, Scorpio, Sagittarius, Capricorn, Aquarius, Pisces.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Essential Dignities Introduction
text: De dignitatibus essentialibus planetarum. CAP. III. PLANETAE in aliquibus zodiaci locis suas vires ac potestates magis exerunt, quam in aliis. & in his propterea fortiores ac fortunatiores esse dicuntur. quod fit, quia natura illorum, vel stellarum in his ipsis locis existentium, naturae planetarum sit convenientior: sitque alterius ad alterum mutua naturae affinitas: unde etiam planetarum essentiales dignitates prisci vocarunt. Cuius generis sunt domus, exaltatio, triplicitas, terminus, & facies eis dicatae. Ac de earum singulis dicendum,
translation: Of the essential dignities of the planets. CHAPTER III. Planets exert their strengths and powers more in some places of the zodiac than in others. And in these places, therefore, they are said to be stronger and more fortunate. This happens because the nature of those places, or of the stars existing in these very places, is more suitable to the nature of the planets: and there is a mutual affinity of nature from one to the other: whence the ancients also called these the essential dignities of the planets. Of this kind are house, exaltation, triplicity, term, and face dedicated to them. And of each of these, we must speak.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Right and Oblique Ascending Signs
text: Secundo in recte, & oblique ascendentia dividuntur. Signa recte ascendentia sunt ♋, ♌, ♍, ♎, ♏, ♐. Signa oblique ascendentia sunt ♑, ♒, ♓, ♈, ♉, ♊. Dicuntur autem recte oriri sive ascendere: quoniam cum eis maior pars aequatoris quam zodiaci peroritur sive ascendit. Quae oblique ascendere sive oriri dicuntur: quia cum eis aequatoris medietas non ascendit: maior enim arcus zodiaci quam aequatoris peroritur. Inferiora sex oblique ascendentia, superioribus recte ascendentibus obediunt, hoc est, ex duobus signis aequidistantibus principio, vel, oblique ascendens recte ascendenti obedit.
translation: Secondly, they are divided into right and oblique ascending signs. Right ascending signs are Cancer, Leo, Virgo, Libra, Scorpio, Sagittarius. Oblique ascending signs are Capricorn, Aquarius, Pisces, Aries, Taurus, Gemini. They are said to rise or ascend directly because with them a greater part of the equator than of the zodiac rises or ascends. Those are said to ascend or rise obliquely because with them half of the equator does not ascend: for a greater arc of the zodiac than of the equator rises. The lower six oblique ascending signs obey the upper right ascending signs, that is, from two signs equidistant from the beginning, or, an oblique ascending sign obeys a right ascending sign.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Four Triplicities
text: Tertio in quatuor triplicitates secantur aeream, igneam, terream & aqueam. Igneae triplicitatis sunt ♈, ♌, ♐. Quae sunt calida & sicca biliofa, amara, diurna, masculina & orientalia. Terreae triplicitatis sunt ♉, ♍, ♑. Quae sunt frigida & sicca melancholica, acida, nocturna, feminina, & meridionalia. Aereae triplicitatis sunt ♊, ♎, ♒. Quae sunt calida & humida sanguinea, dulcia, diurna masculina & occidentalia.
translation: Thirdly, they are divided into four triplicities: airy, fiery, earthy, and watery. Fiery triplicity signs are Aries, Leo, Sagittarius. These are hot and dry, bilious, bitter, diurnal, masculine, and oriental. Earthy triplicity signs are Taurus, Virgo, Capricorn. These are cold and dry, melancholic, acidic, nocturnal, feminine, and meridional. Airy triplicity signs are Gemini, Libra, Aquarius. These are hot and humid, sanguine, sweet, diurnal, masculine, and occidental.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Watery Triplicity
text: Aqueae triplicitatis sunt ♋, ♏, ♓. Quae sunt frigida & humida pituitosa, insipida, feminina, nocturna, & septentrionalia.
translation: Watery triplicity signs are Cancer, Scorpio, Pisces. These are cold and humid, phlegmatic, insipid, feminine, nocturnal, and septentrional (northern).

author: Claude Dariot
title: Cardinal, Fixed, Mutable Signs
text: Quarto in mobilia fixa, & communia sive media dividuntur. Mobilia sunt ♈, ♋, ♎, ♑. Sic dicta: quoniam qui ea in ascendente, hoc est prima domo hora nativitatis habuerit eorum complexionem tortuebitur: vel quia eorum tempora sint mobilia. Fixa sunt ♉, ♌, ♏, ♒. Sic dicta: quia illorum tempora sint fixa, vel quia homines in quorum nativitate orientem sive primam domum tenent, sint fixi, ac in sermonibus factisque stabiles. Communia sive media sunt ♊, ♍, ♐, ♓. Quae hoc nomine vocantur: quia illorum tempora anni, quidpiam cum praecedenti & sequenti commune habent vel quia, ut dictum est, homines in quorum nativitate ascendunt, in sermonibus factisque communes sunt.
translation: Fourthly, they are divided into cardinal, fixed, and common or mutable signs. Cardinal signs are Aries, Cancer, Libra, Capricorn. So called because those who have them in the ascendant, that is, the first house at the hour of birth, will have their temperament twisted: or because their seasons are changeable. Fixed signs are Taurus, Leo, Scorpio, Aquarius. So called because their seasons are fixed, or because people in whose nativity they hold the ascendant or first house are fixed and stable in their words and deeds. Common or mutable signs are Gemini, Virgo, Sagittarius, Pisces. Which are called by this name because their seasons of the year have something in common with the preceding and following, or because, as has been said, people in whose nativity they ascend are common in their words and deeds.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Cardinal Directions, Gender, Day/Night Signs
text: Signorum demum alia orientalia sunt, alia meridionalia, alia occidentalia, & alia borealia. Item alia masculina, feminina vero alia; denique alia diurna, & nocturna alia vocantur. Quae autem ea sint, cum de triplicitatibus sermonem fecimus, docuimus. Dicuntur autem orientalia, meridionalia, occidentalia, & borealia, quod has mundi partes respiciant. Alia vero masculina, quoniam si in mulieris nativitate primam domum habeant, robustioram mulierem reddunt, & virum fortiorem. Feminina contrarium penitus agunt, quia virum effeminat. Diurna demum dicuntur, quoniam pulchriorem & clariorem reddunt natum. Nocturna vero deformiorem, nigrioremque ac obscuram faciem pariunt.
translation: Finally, some signs are oriental, others meridional, others occidental, and others borealian. Likewise, some are masculine, others feminine; finally, some are called diurnal, and others nocturnal. What these are, we have taught when we spoke of the triplicities. They are called oriental, meridional, occidental, and borealian because they respect these parts of the world. Others are masculine, because if they hold the first house in a woman's nativity, they make the woman more robust and the man stronger. Feminine signs act entirely contrary, because they effeminate a man. Diurnal signs, finally, are so called because they make the native more beautiful and brighter. Nocturnal signs, however, produce a more deformed, darker, and obscure face.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Seasonal Quarters of Signs
text: Signa iterum in quatuor quartas, vernalem scilicet, aestivalem, autumnalem & hiemalem dividunt. Quae quartae signa haec anni tempora constituentia comprehendunt ut sequitur: Quarta vernalis quae & sanguinea, dicitur sive puerilis, comprehendit ♈, ♉, ♊. Autumnalis quae melancholiae particeps est, & quae ad tertiam aetatem refertur, continet ♎, ♏, ♐. Aestivalis quarta, quae biliosa ac juvenilis appellatur, haec signa continet ♋, ♌, ♍. Hiemalis, quae senili aetati comparata, pituitamque referens, constituitur ♑, ♒, ♓. Hactenus de zodiaci & signorum divisione dictum est. Nunc ad tractatum de planetis accedo: tum deinde quas in signis dignitates habeant planetae, breviter attingam.
translation: Signs are again divided into four quarters, namely, vernal, aestival, autumnal, and hiemal. These quarters, constituting these seasons of the year, comprise the signs as follows: The vernal quarter, which is also called sanguine or puerile, comprises Aries, Taurus, Gemini. The autumnal quarter, which partakes of melancholy and refers to the third age, contains Libra, Scorpio, Sagittarius. The aestival quarter, which is called bilious and youthful, contains these signs: Cancer, Leo, Virgo. The hiemal quarter, which is compared to old age and refers to phlegm, is constituted by Capricorn, Aquarius, Pisces. So much has been said about the division of the zodiac and signs. Now I proceed to the treatise on the planets: then I will briefly touch upon what dignities the planets have in the signs.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Nature of Saturn
text: A Saturno (quod planetarum omnium superior sit) sumpto initio, de iis secundum sphaerarum ordinem, sermonem faciam. Saturnus itaque frigidus est & siccus, melancholicus, naturae humanae inimicus & vitam destruens, masculinus, diurnus, malevolus & infortuna maior.
translation: Beginning with Saturn (because he is superior to all planets), I will speak of them according to the order of the spheres. Saturn, therefore, is cold and dry, melancholic, an enemy to human nature and destructive of life, masculine, diurnal, malevolent, and the greater infortune.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Nature of Jupiter
text: Iupiter calidus est & humidus, sanguineus, naturae amicus, masculinus, diurnus, & fortuna maior.
translation: Jupiter is hot and humid, sanguine, a friend to nature, masculine, diurnal, and the greater fortune.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Nature of Mars
text: Mars calidus est immoderate & siccus, cholericus, masculinus, nocturnus, malevolus & infortuna minor.
translation: Mars is immoderately hot and dry, choleric, masculine, nocturnal, malevolent, and the lesser infortune.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Nature of the Sun
text: Sol calidus est & siccus moderate, masculinus, diurnus, fortuna per aspectum, & infortuna per corporalem coniunctionem.
translation: The Sun is moderately hot and dry, masculine, diurnal, a fortune by aspect, and an infortune by bodily conjunction.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Chapter 13: Treating Body Parts
text: CAP. X111. VÆ SIGNA &domus corporis humani partes reſpiciant in precedentibus dictum est. Prima enim domus & Aries caput totum gubernant, quemad modum Taurus cum secunda domo collum.Gemini vna cú domo tertia humeris & brachijs vt dictú est, preſidet, quæ summoperè notanda sunt. Partes en nostri corporis tali cõstellatione regnáte medicantur, & à morbis liberantur. Sed morbi qui cura tione egent, aut recentes sunt, aut inveterati. Si igitur morbi inveterati funt:cura Luna exiſtente in tri plicitate terrea, precipuè in Tauro incipienda est. Si verò recens fuerit, in aquaticis signis precipuè purgationis tempore Luna sit. Attamen quoniam vel morbus non finit, vel tempus hoc nolunt ægroti expectare. Que sequuntur tamé summoperè animaduertenda funt. Cùm partem aliquam morbo laborantem ad sanitatem reducere libuerit:fignum partem reſpiciens Luna teneat,aut illud signum aſcendat. Lunam tamen in aſcédente ponere malum est: ibi enim infortunio afficitur. Veluti si caput aut aliquam illius partem curare tentes,aut Luna in Ariete collocabis,aut Arietem aſcédere cogas. Si autem colli partes sanati reſtituédę sunt, aut Taurus aſcé- det, aut eum Luna tenebit. Si demùm humeros manus & brachia à morbis liberare cupis, aut geminos aſcendere cogas, aut eos Lunam tenere tentabis.
translation: Chapter 13: What signs and houses rule parts of the human body has been said in the preceding. For the first house and Aries govern the entire head, just as Taurus with the second house governs the neck. Gemini, together with the third house, presides over the shoulders and arms, as has been said, which are most important to note. Our body parts are treated and freed from diseases when such a constellation rules. But diseases that require treatment are either recent or inveterate. Therefore, if the diseases are inveterate, treatment should begin when the Moon is in an earthy triplicity, especially in Taurus. If, however, it is recent, the Moon should be in watery signs, especially at the time of purgation. Nevertheless, because either the disease does not allow, or the patients do not want to wait for this time, the following things must be especially observed. When one wishes to restore a diseased part to health, the Moon should occupy the sign ruling that part, or that sign should ascend. However, placing the Moon in the Ascendant is bad, for there it is afflicted by misfortune. For example, if you try to treat the head or any part of it, you will either place the Moon in Aries or cause Aries to ascend. If, however, the parts of the neck are to be restored to health, either Taurus will ascend, or the Moon will occupy it. If, finally, you wish to free the shoulders, hands, and arms from diseases, you will either cause Gemini to ascend or try to have the Moon occupy them.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Nature of Venus
text: Venus frigida est & humida temperata, phlegmatica, fortuna minor, feminina, nocturna & bonae naturae.
translation: Venus is cold and moderately humid, phlegmatic, the lesser fortune, feminine, nocturnal, and of good nature.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Chapter 14: Bloodletting - General Rules
text: CAP. XIIII. EN Æ sectio est viuerſalis euacuatio plenitudinem euacuans. Plura igitur in sanguinis miſsione siue venę sectione animaduertenda funt. sed potiſsimum vires corporis, & aëris coſtitutio, & aſtrorum motus, de quibus motibus in preſentia sermo à nobis habe tur. Quòd autem ad corporis conſtitutionem & vires attinet. Pueris ante decimumquartum annum, sanguinem educi haud par est: quemadmodum senibus post septuageſimum, nisi vires valeant. Quidem enim funt qui anno septuageſimo vel etiam octuageſimo eam admittant, quod aliqui sexſagesimo vel quinquagesimo anno ob virum imbecillitatem non faciunt. Macilentis item ac tabidis, vena non est secanda, vt neq; pinguibus:his enim ob pinguedinis copiam à sectione venę vasa in sese concidunt, illis parum admodum est sanguinis qui ad nu tritionem omninò requiritur. Morbi tamen in quibus conueniat aut non animaduerténdi sunt. Morbi enim magni (Galeno libr. quarto methodi teſte) venę sectionem cupiunt, modo vires & ætas permittat. In omnibus etiam morbis, qui ortus sui causam ex sanguine habent, vt funt continens febris & omnis generis phlegmones, miſsio sanguinis proficit. Aër etiam inſpiciendus est, calidúſne aut siccus sit, aut frigidus,an verò temperatus. Quando enim aër vel ob eius conſtitutionem, vel anni tempus, vel regionem, nimis calidusvel frigidus fuerit, mittédus haud est sanguis:quia calidum nimiŭ diſsſoluit, frigidum autem nimium refrigerat. Itaque aër optimè tempe ratus sit, & in omnium qualitatum medio conſtitutus cùm sanguis mittitur. Aër demùm non sit nebulofus, sed serenus. Nec tunc temporis ventus auſtrinus perflare debet, sed aquilonius aut zephirus fauonius vtpote qui ab oriente initium sumens temperatus sit. Sed hec à medicis satis supérq; docentur:quare ad propofitum adeamus.
translation: Chapter 14: Bloodletting is a universal evacuation that empties plenitude. Therefore, several things must be observed in bloodletting, or venesection, but especially the body's strength, the constitution of the air, and the movements of the stars, about which movements we are now speaking. As for the body's constitution and strength: it is not proper to draw blood from children before the fourteenth year, nor from the elderly after the seventieth, unless their strength permits. Indeed, there are some who allow it in their seventieth or even eightieth year, which some do not do in their sixtieth or fiftieth year due to weakness. Likewise, veins should not be cut in emaciated and consumptive individuals, nor in the obese: for in the latter, due to an abundance of fat, the vessels collapse during venesection; in the former, there is very little blood, which is absolutely necessary for nutrition. However, diseases in which it is suitable or not must be observed. For great diseases (as Galen testifies in the fourth book of Methodus) demand venesection, provided strength and age permit. In all diseases that have their origin in the blood, such as continuous fever and all kinds of phlegmons, bloodletting is beneficial. The air must also be inspected, whether it is hot or dry, or cold, or temperate. For when the air, either due to its constitution, the time of year, or the region, is too hot or too cold, blood should not be drawn: because too much heat dissolves, and too much cold refrigerates. Therefore, the air should be optimally temperate and constituted in the middle of all qualities when blood is drawn. Finally, the air should not be cloudy, but clear. Nor should the south wind blow at that time, but the north wind or the west wind (zephyrus favonius), which, taking its origin from the east, is temperate. But these things are taught sufficiently and more by physicians; therefore, let us proceed to our purpose.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Nature of Mercury
text: Mercurius in omnibus communis est, & convertibilis, masculinus cum masculinis, femininus cum femininis, cum calidis calidus, cum humidis humidus fortuna cum fortunis praecipue laudabili aspectu vel corpore coniunctus aut applicans.
translation: Mercury is common in all things, and convertible; masculine with masculine planets, feminine with feminine, hot with hot, humid with humid; a fortune with fortunes, especially when conjoined or applying by a laudable aspect or body.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Chapter 15: Baths - Moon in Signs
text: CAP. XV. VONIAM (vt iam sepiſsimè dictum est) Luna secundum loci qualitatem in quo est, in hęc inferiora influit. Ideò qa signa terrea frigida sunt & sicca, ac illoru est costringere: Luna in illis, hoc est in Tauro, Virgine & Capricorno exiſtente, tempus ad balneű ido neum non est. Aptum aut erit balneo diffoluēte: vti Luna in Ariete, Leone & Sagittario exiſtente:que signa calida sunt & sicca, ac calidi fit diffoluere. Cùm verò humectare desideratur, in Gemellis vel Libra quę signa sunt aërea Luna sit, non autemin Aquario.quoniam est Saturni domus. Quando tamen in brachijs, AVTHO. CLAV. DARIOTO MEDI. 111 humeris vel manibus apponendę sunt cucurbitulę, Gemini vitandi sunt, qui alio tempore vt etiam Aquarius conueniunt. Signa demùm frigida & humida, vt sunt Cancer, Scorpius & Pisces eligun tur : cùm refrigeratio & humectatio desiderantur. Cùm etiam diffoluere cupimus, conueniunt: humidum enim humores ad diſsolutionem præparat, qui poſthac balneo diſsſoluuntur.
translation: Chapter 15: Since (as has been very often said) the Moon influences these lower things according to the quality of the place in which it is. Therefore, because earthy signs are cold and dry, and their nature is to constrict: when the Moon is in them, that is, in Taurus, Virgo, and Capricorn, it is not a suitable time for a bath. But it will be suitable for a dissolving bath when the Moon is in Aries, Leo, and Sagittarius, which are hot and dry signs, and it is the nature of heat to dissolve. When, however, moistening is desired, the Moon should be in Gemini or Libra, which are airy signs, but not in Aquarius, because it is Saturn's house. However, when cupping glasses are to be applied to the arms, shoulders, or hands, Gemini should be avoided, which at other times, like Aquarius, are suitable. Finally, cold and moist signs, such as Cancer, Scorpio, and Pisces, are chosen when cooling and moistening are desired. They are also suitable when we wish to dissolve, for moisture prepares the humors for dissolution, which are afterwards dissolved by the bath.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Nature of the Moon
text: Luna denique frigida est & humida, quamvis parum calefaciat: magis tamen humectat. Naturam suam sive qualitatem per quartas suas, non secus ac Sol in quartis & anni temporibus commutat. Feminina est etiam & nocturna, & omnium virtutum & impressionum aliorum planetarum delatrix. Nam omnes influentiae per orbem ipsius ad nos ultimo descendunt. Haec de naturis planetarum quod ad institutum nostrum attinet, satis dictum sit.
translation: Finally, the Moon is cold and humid, although it heats but little: yet it moistens more. It changes its nature or quality through its quarters, just as the Sun changes in the quarters and seasons of the year. It is also feminine and nocturnal, and the bearer of all virtues and impressions of the other planets. For all influences ultimately descend to us through its orb. So much has been said about the natures of the planets as pertains to our purpose.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Erecting Houses: Midday
text: ETSI diversa sint tempora ad quae figurae caelestes erigi possunt: tamen ea ad duo reducimus. Meridianum scilicet, & pomeridianum. Tempus autem pomeridianum sumitur a meridie diei unius ad alterius diei sequentis meridiem: hoc est numerando ab una hora usque ad horas quatuor & viginti. Primo igitur de domorum erectione in meridie, quoniam minus difficultatis habet, dicemus: deinde in pomeridiano tempore. Si igitur meridiei tempore caelithema sive domos erigere placet: delineata prius (astronomico more) figura, locus Solis meridiei propositi in ephemeridum tabulis perquiratur. Invenietur autem in directo diei tui propositi & in tabula tuo anno serviente. Ipse autem gradus inventus, cum minutis (si quae sint) in decimae domus principio sive cuspide ponatur, una cum signi charactere sub quo tunc Sol existit. Hoc facto quaeratur inter
translation: Although there are different times at which celestial figures can be erected, we reduce them to two: namely, midday and post-meridian. The post-meridian time is taken from midday of one day to midday of the following day, that is, counting from one hour up to twenty-four hours. First, therefore, we will speak of the erection of houses at midday, since it has less difficulty; then for post-meridian time. If, therefore, you wish to erect the celestial chart or houses at midday: first, having drawn the figure (in the astronomical manner), the place of the Sun for the proposed midday is sought in the ephemeris tables. It will be found directly for your proposed day and in the table serving your year. That degree found, with its minutes (if there are any), is placed at the beginning or cusp of the tenth house, along with the character of the sign under which the Sun then exists. This done, let it be sought among

author: Claude Dariot
title: Nature of Lunar Nodes
text: Sed capitis & caudae draconis naturas hic annectere, non mihi praeter institutum videtur. Caput igitur draconis veluti Mercurius cum bonis bonum est, cum malo malum. In coniunctione namque bonorum bonitatem, malorum vero eorum malitiam auget. Cauda vero capiti penitus adversatur: cum bonis enim mala est, & cum malis bona: ita ut boni bonitatem, & mali malitiam minuat. Omnes autem hii
translation: But to annex here the natures of the Dragon's Head and Tail does not seem to me beyond my purpose. The Dragon's Head, therefore, like Mercury, is good with good planets, and bad with bad. For in conjunction with benefics, it increases their goodness, but with malefics, it increases their malice. The Tail, however, is entirely contrary to the Head: for with benefics it is bad, and with malefics it is good: so that it diminishes the goodness of the good, and the malice of the bad.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Placing Planets in the Chart
text: RECTA figura planetae omnes una cum capite & cauda draconis, in ipsa aptendi sunt: id quod hac ratione fiet. Locus planetae in ephemeride hora proposita inventus, in domibus perquiratur: quo reperto, ibi planetam locabis. Sed signum loci planetae aliquando (idque saepissime) in aliqua domo notatur, in qua non est planeta ponendus: quoniam gradus loci ipsius in alia sunt: in qua (ut exemplo sequenti patebit) planeta cum gradu minuto atque signi, in quo inventus fuit, charactere, collocandus est. Character autem signi scribitur, cum in domus principio aliud signum scriptum est.
translation: In the erected figure, all planets, along with the head and tail of the Dragon, are to be placed in it; this will be done in the following way. The place of the planet found in the ephemeris at the proposed hour is sought in the houses: once found, you will place the planet there. But the sign of the planet's place is sometimes (and very often) noted in a house where the planet is not to be placed, because the degrees of its place are in another house, in which (as will be clear from the following example) the planet, with its degree, minute, and the character of the sign in which it was found, is to be placed. The character of the sign is written when another sign is written at the beginning of the house.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Planetary and Nodal Symbols
text: planetae, caput & cauda draconis characteribus quibusdam propriis figurantur: qui se in hunc modum habent. ♄, ♃, ♂, ☉, ♀, ☿, ☽. Saturnus, Iupiter, Mars, Sol, Venus, Mercurius, Luna. ☊, ☋. Caput Draconis, Cauda Draconis.
translation: planets, the Head and Tail of the Dragon are figured by certain proper characters: which are as follows. Saturn ♄, Jupiter ♃, Mars ♂, Sun ☉, Venus ♀, Mercury ☿, Moon ☽. Dragon's Head ☊, Dragon's Tail ☋.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Calculating Arabic Parts
text: positae sunt, cum capite & cauda Draconis. Caput in ephemeride notatur, sed non cauda: verum ea semper in oppositione capitis est. De partibus quibusdam, earumque inventione. CAP. XVIII. Pars fortunae similiter in figura aptanda est, & si de aegro quaestio fiat, pars vitae una cum mortis & planetae interficientis partibus, ut postea dicetur in tertio libro. Sed tamen earum inventionem in praesentiarum docebimus. Pars vitae diei tempore a Jove in Saturnum sumitur. Noctis autem tempore contra, & ab ascendente utroque tempore projicitur. Pars fortunae, in die a Sole in Lunam, in nocte autem a Luna in Solem sumitur, & ut pars vitae tam diei quam noctis tempore ascendenti additur. Pars mortis, nocte dieque a Luna in gradum domus octavae capitur: ac a signo in quo est Saturnus, projicitur. Planetae interficientis, diei tempore a domino ascendentis in Lunam numeratur. Contra vero a Luna in dominum ascendentis accipitur, & ab ascendente projicitur. Cum ergo partis alicuius locum indagare placuerit, reductis prius signis ad numeros ordine, ita ut Aries primum locum teneat: Taurus vero secundum, ac sic deinceps ita ut Pisces duodecim reperiantur, sequitur. Aries 1, Taurus 2, Gemini 3, Cancer 4, Leo 5, Virgo 6, Libra 7, Scorpio 8, Sagittarius 9, Capricornus 10, Aquarius 11, Pisces 12.
translation: are placed, with the head and tail of the Dragon. The Head is noted in the ephemeris, but not the Tail: but it is always in opposition to the Head. Of certain Parts, and their invention. CAP. XVIII. The Part of Fortune is similarly to be placed in the figure, and if a question is made about a sick person, the Part of Life along with the Parts of Death and the killing planet, as will be said later in the third book. But we will teach their invention for the present. The Part of Life is taken in daytime from Jupiter to Saturn. In nighttime, however, the reverse, and it is projected from the Ascendant at both times. The Part of Fortune, in the day, is taken from the Sun to the Moon; in the night, however, from the Moon to the Sun, and like the Part of Life, it is added to the Ascendant both day and night. The Part of Death, day and night, is taken from the Moon to the degree of the eighth house; and it is projected from the sign in which Saturn is. The Part of the killing planet, in daytime, is counted from the lord of the Ascendant to the Moon. Conversely, however, it is taken from the Moon to the lord of the Ascendant, and projected from the Ascendant. Therefore, when it pleases to investigate the place of any part, first reducing the signs to numbers in order, so that Aries holds the first place: Taurus the second, and so on, so that Pisces is found to be the twelfth, it follows: Aries 1, Taurus 2, Gemini 3, Cancer 4, Leo 5, Virgo 6, Libra 7, Scorpio 8, Sagittarius 9, Capricorn 10, Aquarius 11, Pisces 12.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Houses of Luminaries
text: De prima dignitate essentiali, scilicet de domo. SOL atque Luna luminaria duo a Deo condita, unus ut diei, alter ut nocti praeesset: quia in rerum ac corporum generatione & dominatu principatum obtinent, illis pro domibus tributa sunt duo signa suis qualitatibus convenientia, atque naturis. Nempe Lunae Cancer, Soli vero Leo, signa certe nostro vertici propinquiora, ac maiorem aestatis parte constituunt.
translation: Of the first essential dignity, namely, the house. The Sun and Moon, two luminaries created by God, one to preside over the day, the other over the night: because they hold primacy in the generation and dominion of things and bodies, two signs suitable to their qualities and natures are assigned to them as houses. Namely, Cancer for the Moon, and Leo for the Sun, signs certainly closer to our zenith, and constituting the greater part of summer.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Preliminary Horary Observations
text: Jam vero (erecta figura, ac in ipsa planetis collocatis cum partibus ut monstratum est) judicium de re proposita daturi, animadvertenda quaedam sunt antequam proferatur judicium, ne in eo sit error. Primo itaque septima domus ac eius dominus animadvertendi sunt: quoniam si septima domus impedita fuerit malorum aspectu vel praesentia, aut eius dominus impeditus fuerit, hoc est fuerit vel in suo casu, vel retrogradus aut combustus, aut alio modo impeditus infortuniis jam antea praedictis, tuto non est judicandum, ne error judicando committatur. Nam Ptolemaeus verbo 14 centiloquii inquit. Quantis in erroribus versatur astrologus, cum locus septimus ac eius dominus afflicti sunt. Praeterea animadvertendum est diligenter, an quaestio sit radicalis necne. Id quod cognoscitur, si dominus ascendentis & dominus horae idem sint, vel eiusdem triplicitatis aut naturae. Existente enim hac constellatione, radicalis est quaestio. Contra vero minime: ideo de absoluto judicio dubitandum est.
translation: Now indeed (the figure having been erected, and the planets placed in it with the parts as shown), when about to give judgment on the proposed matter, certain things must be observed before judgment is pronounced, lest there be an error in it. First, therefore, the seventh house and its lord must be observed: because if the seventh house is impeded by the aspect or presence of malefics, or its lord is impeded, that is, if it is in its fall, or retrograde, or combust, or impeded in any other way by the misfortunes already predicted, it is not safe to judge, lest an error be committed in judging. For Ptolemy says in the 14th aphorism of his Centiloquium: 'In how many errors does the astrologer find himself when the seventh house and its lord are afflicted.' Furthermore, it must be diligently observed whether the question is radical or not. This is known if the lord of the Ascendant and the lord of the hour are the same, or of the same triplicity or nature. For when this constellation exists, the question is radical. Conversely, it is not at all: therefore, one must doubt an absolute judgment.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Qualities of Luminaries' Houses
text: constituentia. Qualitatibus autem sive naturis inter se conveniunt: quia Cancer frigidus est & humidus, femininus, nocturnus & mobilis ut Luna. Leo vero signum est calidum & siccum, masculinum, fixum, & diurnum ut ipse Sol. Supradicti planetae unicum signum habent pro domo. Reliqui autem quinque signa duo possident, unum scilicet diurnum, alterum nocturnum. Unde signum diurnum domus diurna dicitur. Nocturnum vero nocturna.
translation: constituting. However, they agree in qualities or natures among themselves: because Cancer is cold and humid, feminine, nocturnal, and cardinal like the Moon. Leo, however, is a sign that is hot and dry, masculine, fixed, and diurnal like the Sun itself. The aforementioned planets have a single sign as their house. The remaining five planets, however, possess two signs, one diurnal and the other nocturnal. Hence, a diurnal sign is called a diurnal house. And a nocturnal sign, a nocturnal house.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Identifying Significators
text: PRIUSQUAM ad judicia veniamus, de significatoribus discendum est: utpote quinam, & quid sint. Est igitur significator, tum domus rem significans, tum planeta plures habens in ea domo dignitates: qui dominus domus appellatur, de cuius inventione in precedentibus dictum est. Cum igitur aliqua in medium adfertur quaestio: ascendens cum suo rectore dandus est querenti, aut quaestionis authori. Domus autem rem quaesitam significans, ac eius dominus, rei de qua quaeritur, tribuendus est. Luna vero omnibus quaestionibus communis, querenti semper cum ascendenti ac eius domino tribuenda venit. (ut exempli causa) si aliquis de fratris statu quaestionem moveat: ascendens & ascendentis dominus, & Luna querenti. Tertia vero ac eius dominus fratri suo dabitur. Vel si quis quaerat de cuiusdam hominis aegritudine: ascendens eius dominus & Luna laboranti: sexta vero cum suo domino, morbo sunt tribuendi. Ac sic de aliis quaestionibus est agendum. Interim tamen sciendum est, aliquando significatores multos accipiendos esse: quemadmodum in his rebus quae fiunt intervenientibus legatis, vel aliquibus aliis: ut venditiones legatis, vel pacis tractationes, conjugia, & plura similia. In his enim cum ascendente eius domino & Luna, miscendus est planeta, a quo separatur Luna. Planeta autem cui applicat Luna, rei de qua quaeritur cum aliis significatoribus suis supradictis dandus est. Additur etiam ipsis significatoribus planeta qui cum rei significatione, aliquid commune habet. Ut (exempli causa) quando de conjugio futuro aliquis quaestionem proponit: querenti ascendens dominus ascendentis & Luna, ac planeta a quo ipsa separatur, dandi sunt: septimam vero domum, eius dominum ac planetam cui Luna applicat, ei de quo quaeritur dabis. Si autem vir querens fuerit, significatoribus suis Solem adjunge: quia Sol virum significat. Significatoribus autem illius de quo quaeritur Venerem tribuas. Si vero mulier quaestionem faciat, significatoribus suis Venerem, quae mulierem regit: aliis vero Solem praebebis. Atque sic de aliis faciendum est. Habitus enim illorum tum ad invicem, tum ad fortunatos aut infortunatos, quid de re futurum sit, ostendit. Quare de significatoribus hactenus dictum sit: ad judicia fauste properandum videtur.
translation: Before we come to judgments, we must learn about significators: namely, who and what they are. A significator, therefore, is both the house signifying the matter, and the planet that has many dignities in that house, which is called the lord of the house, about whose invention we have spoken in the preceding sections. Therefore, when any question is brought forward: the Ascendant with its ruler is to be given to the querent, or the author of the question. The house signifying the matter sought, and its lord, is to be attributed to the matter about which the question is asked. The Moon, common to all questions, is always to be attributed to the querent along with the Ascendant and its lord. (For example) if someone asks a question about the state of his brother: the Ascendant and the lord of the Ascendant, and the Moon, are for the querent. The third house and its lord will be given to his brother. Or if someone asks about the illness of a certain person: the Ascendant, its lord, and the Moon are for the sick person; the sixth house with its lord are to be attributed to the illness. And so it is to be done for other questions. Meanwhile, however, it must be known that sometimes many significators are to be taken: as in those matters that occur with the intervention of envoys, or some others: such as sales, peace treaties, marriages, and many similar things. In these, with the Ascendant, its lord, and the Moon, the planet from which the Moon separates is to be mixed in. The planet to which the Moon applies is to be given to the matter about which the question is asked, along with its other aforesaid significators. Also added to these significators is the planet that has something in common with the signification of the matter. For example, when someone proposes a question about a future marriage: the Ascendant, the lord of the Ascendant, and the Moon, and the planet from which she separates, are to be given to the querent: but the seventh house, its lord, and the planet to which the Moon applies, you will give to the person about whom the question is asked. If the querent is a man, add the Sun to his significators, because the Sun signifies a man. To the significators of the person about whom the question is asked, attribute Venus. If, however, a woman asks the question, to her significators, Venus, who rules women; to the others, you will give the Sun. And so it is to be done for others. For their condition, both among themselves and with benefics or malefics, shows what will happen concerning the matter. Therefore, enough has been said about significators: it seems appropriate to proceed auspiciously to judgments.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Houses of Saturn
text: Saturnus itaque quia naturae est inimicus, oppositas domos obtinuit, Capricornum scilicet & Aquarium. Capricornus enim domus est nocturna, Aquarius vero diurna.
translation: Saturn, therefore, because he is an enemy to nature, obtained opposite houses, namely Capricorn and Aquarius. For Capricorn is a nocturnal house, and Aquarius is diurnal.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Four Ways of Perfection
text: ABSOLUTO sermone de principiis huius scientiae & observationibus necessariis: Nunc recta praeceptorum series, nos ad rectam rerum efficiendarum rationem tradendam invitat. Dicamus igitur quot modis res omnes perficiuntur. Quod quidem contingit quatuor fieri modis. Conjunctione scilicet, sive congressu, luminis & naturae translatione, collectione luminis, & in domibus existentia. Cum ergo dominus ascendentis, significator rei quaesitae & Luna junguntur, vel per conjunctionem, vel aspectum: res omnino absolvitur: praecipue si in ascendente conjunctio fiat. Fit tamen cum difficultate aliqua, quando in domo rem quaesitam significante conjunguntur. Secundo luminis & naturae translatione absolvitur, id est si praedicti significatores non jungantur conjunctione, neque aspectu adest aliquis planeta qui ab uno significatorum separetur, fueritque ab eo receptus, & alteri significatori applicetur, antequam alicui alteri applicet: quia tunc unum & naturam ab alio acceptam in alterum transfert: is enim qui lumen recipit, perfectionis rei author est. Hac ratione cum evenit,
translation: Having finished speaking about the principles of this science and necessary observations: now the direct series of precepts invites us to deliver the correct method for bringing matters to completion. Let us therefore say in how many ways all matters are brought to perfection. This indeed happens in four ways: namely, by conjunction or congress, by translation of light and nature, by collection of light, and by existence in the houses. Therefore, when the lord of the Ascendant, the significator of the matter sought, and the Moon are joined, either by conjunction or aspect, the matter is entirely brought to completion, especially if the conjunction occurs in the Ascendant. It happens, however, with some difficulty when they are joined in the house signifying the matter sought. Secondly, it is brought to completion by translation of light and nature, that is, if the aforesaid significators are not joined by conjunction or aspect, there is some planet present which separates from one of the significators, and has been received by it, and applies to the other significator, before it applies to any other: because then it transfers the light and nature received from one to the other. For he who receives the light is the author of the perfection of the matter. By this method it happens,

author: Claude Dariot
title: Houses of Jupiter
text: Iupiter vero quoniam naturae est parens & amicus domos suas habuit, Solis & Lunae domos trino aspectu aspicientes: qui quia Saturnum subsequitur, signum unum ante Saturni domos, alterum vero post, Sagittarius scilicet & Pisces obtinuit. Sagittarius diurna domus est, Pisces vero nocturna.
translation: Jupiter, however, because he is a parent and friend to nature, had his houses, looking at the houses of the Sun and Moon with a trine aspect: and because he follows Saturn, he obtained one sign before Saturn's houses and another after, namely Sagittarius and Pisces. Sagittarius is a diurnal house, and Pisces is nocturnal.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Absent Person: Dead or Alive
text: MULTAE ac variae quaestiones in medium adferri solent, quae ad aliquam domorum duodecim referuntur, secundum ipsarum significationes. Verum quia uniuscuiusque domus, innumeras fere significationes possidet. Si eas omnes persequi & in unum volumen cogere optaremus: ingens noster labor facile excresceret. Quare, proposita et in medium adducta de unaquaque domo unica quaestione, de reliquis (modo percipiantur praedicta) facile judicabitur. Prima itaque domus de vita discernit: ideo quaestio de absente quodam proposita, an scilicet mortuus sit, aut vivat, ad primam domum refertur. Cum ergo talem quaestionem solvere cupis: vide an dominus ascendentis, Luna et octavae domus dominus, simul juncti sint, illud enim hominem e vivis discessisse arguit. Vide item an sit translatio luminis domini ascendentis ad octavae dominum, aut contra: vel an sit octavae dominus in ascendente, vel ascendentis dominus in angulo terrae sit collocatus. Omnia enim haec hominem vitam finivisse dicunt. Animadvertendus est praeterea habitus domini ascendentis cum fortunatis aut infortunatis: utpote quod applicationes cum bonis vitam: cum malis vero detrimentum mortemque adferunt. Idem de ascendente ac parte fortunae judicandum est. Praesentia enim malorum in ascendente, vel cum parte fortunae mortem adfert, bonorum vero vitam.
translation: Many and varied questions are usually brought forward, which relate to one of the twelve houses, according to their significations. But because each house possesses almost innumerable significations, if we wished to pursue them all and gather them into one volume, our immense labor would easily grow. Therefore, having proposed and brought forward a single question for each house, the rest will be easily judged (provided the aforesaid are understood). The first house, therefore, discerns about life: hence a question proposed about an absent person, whether he is dead or alive, refers to the first house. Therefore, when you wish to solve such a question: see if the lord of the Ascendant, the Moon, and the lord of the eighth house are joined together, for that argues that the person has departed from the living. See also if there is a translation of light from the lord of the Ascendant to the lord of the eighth, or vice versa: or if the lord of the eighth is in the Ascendant, or the lord of the Ascendant is placed in an angle of the earth (4th house). For all these things say that the person has ended his life. Furthermore, the condition of the lord of the Ascendant with benefics or malefics must be observed: inasmuch as applications with benefics bring life; with malefics, however, detriment and death. The same is to be judged concerning the Ascendant and the Part of Fortune. For the presence of malefics in the Ascendant, or with the Part of Fortune, brings death; of benefics, however, life.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Houses of Mars
text: Nunc autem Mars proxime subsequitur, qui fere veluti Saturnus naturae est inimicus: ita ipsius domus Solis & Lunae domos aspectu inimicitiae imperfecte quadrato, scilicet respiciunt. Sunt igitur Martis domus, Arietis & Scorpionis signa. Aries diurna, Scorpius vero nocturna.
translation: Now Mars follows next, who is almost an enemy to nature like Saturn: thus his houses look at the houses of the Sun and Moon with an aspect of imperfect enmity, namely a square. Therefore, the houses of Mars are the signs of Aries and Scorpio. Aries is diurnal, and Scorpio is nocturnal.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Acquiring Possessions & Timing
text: CUM QUIS in medium quaestionem adfert, an possit substantiam quandam possidere: & si id fieri possit, quando illud fiet scire desiderat: nec tamen dicere velit, a quo se habiturum sperat. Tamen responsum daturi videndum est, an sit applicatio quaedam inter dominum domus secundae, Lunam & dominum ascendentis: vel si non sit applicatio, videndum est an sit translatio luminis & naturae inter eos: vel si eorum lumen ab aliquo superiori colligatur, aut dominus secundae domus sit in ascendente fortunatus, ac parte fortunae respiciens, vel ascendentis dominus fortunatus sit & in secunda domo situs: quoniam si illorum aliquod, aut plures fuerint, querentem substantiam habiturum fore denotent. Si autem non reperiantur, contrarium eveniet: nisi Luna in domo secunda sedem habeat. Tempus autem quo id eveniet scies, si gradibus singulis interceptis inter eorum conjunctionem vel aspectum ad Jovem vel partem fortunae, aut unius significatoris ad alterum, diem unum, hebdomadam, mensem vel annum secundum signi qualitatem tribueris. Signa enim mobilia horas aut dies significant: communia vero menses vel hebdomadas, & fixa annos aut menses tribuunt. Hac ergo ratione tempus quo res aliqua quaesita eveniet, cognoscitur.
translation: When someone brings forward a question, whether he can possess some substance: and if it can happen, he desires to know when it will happen: yet he does not wish to say from whom he hopes to receive it. Nevertheless, when giving a response, it must be seen whether there is some application between the lord of the second house, the Moon, and the lord of the Ascendant: or if there is no application, it must be seen whether there is a translation of light and nature between them: or if their light is collected by some superior planet, or if the lord of the second house is fortunate in the Ascendant, and aspects the Part of Fortune, or if the lord of the Ascendant is fortunate and situated in the second house: because if any of these, or several, are present, they denote that the querent will acquire the substance. If, however, they are not found, the opposite will happen: unless the Moon has its seat in the second house. The time when this will happen you will know, if for each degree intercepted between their conjunction or aspect to Jupiter or the Part of Fortune, or from one significator to another, you attribute one day, week, month, or year according to the quality of the sign. For cardinal signs signify hours or days; mutable signs, however, months or weeks; and fixed signs attribute years or months. By this method, therefore, the time when any sought matter will happen is known.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Houses of Venus
text: Veneris praeterea domus sunt, Taurus & Libra: Taurus nocturna, Libra diurna. Atque ut est bonae benignaeque naturae domus eius Solis ac Lunae domos sextili, hoc est imperfectae amicitiae aspectu respiciunt.
translation: Furthermore, the houses of Venus are Taurus and Libra: Taurus is nocturnal, Libra is diurnal. And as she is of a good and benevolent nature, her houses look at the houses of the Sun and Moon with a sextile aspect, that is, an aspect of imperfect friendship.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Inquiring About Siblings
text: RES ITA SE HABENT in rerum natura, ut complexiones hominum qualitatesque variae sint. Quidam civitates ac regiones perpetuo discurrunt: alii vero nunquam vel raro e sua patria & sua sede dimoventur. Viatoribus autem & terras peragrantibus varia accidentia evenire solent: unde etiam variae de illis quaestiones proponuntur. Cum ergo a quodam de fratris sui habitu quaestio movebitur: videnda est tertia domus, quae de fratribus judicat: et animadvertendus est eius dominus, et dominus triplicitatis. Quoniam illorum habitus cum fortunatis vel infortunatis, qualis eius dispositio sit ostendit. Si enim tertiae dominus in sexta domo fuerit, vel dominus sextae in tertia, aut unus applicationem aspectumve cum altero habuerit: ipsum morbo teneri arguunt. Si vero fratris significator in septima domo fuerit, vel Luna, aut septimae dominus in tertia, aut applicatio inter eos fuerit, ipsum uxorem duxisse vel ducere indicant. Si autem hae dispositiones cum domino octavae domus, aut ipsa domo fuerint, vel tertiae dominus in quarta domo fuerit, eum mortuum esse significant. Applicatio item vel aspectus aut conjunctio significatorum fratris cum fortunatis eum bene valere indicant: cum infortunatis vero contrarium agunt. Hoc modo de habitu significatorum fratris cum aliorum domorum dominis, atque de aliis quaestionibus propositis sive de patre, sive de matre, sive de uxore, sive de filiis, vel de aliis quae variae et diversae esse possunt, judicandum est.
translation: Matters in the nature of things are such that the complexions and qualities of people are varied. Some wander perpetually through cities and regions; others, however, never or rarely move from their homeland and their seat. Various accidents usually happen to travelers and those traversing lands: hence various questions are proposed about them. Therefore, when a question is moved by someone about the condition of his brother: the third house, which judges about brothers, must be seen, and its lord must be observed, and the lord of the triplicity. For their condition with benefics or malefics shows what his disposition is. If the lord of the third is in the sixth house, or the lord of the sixth in the third, or one has an application or aspect with the other: they argue that he is afflicted by illness. If, however, the significators of the brother are in the seventh house, or the Moon, or the lord of the seventh in the third, or there is an application between them, they indicate that he has married or will marry. If, however, these dispositions are with the lord of the eighth house, or the house itself, or the lord of the third is in the fourth house, they signify that he is dead. An application or aspect or conjunction of the brother's significators with benefics indicates that he is well: with malefics, however, they act to the contrary. In this way, judgment is to be made concerning the condition of the brother's significators with the lords of other houses, and concerning other proposed questions, whether about the father, or the mother, or the wife, or the children, or others that can be varied and diverse.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Houses of Mercury
text: Ultimo subsequitur Mercurius, cuius domus sunt Gemini & Virgo. Gemini diurna, Virgo autem nocturna. Quae quoniam Mercurius naturae communis existit, nullo aspectu cum Solis ac Lunae domibus coniunguntur.
translation: Lastly, Mercury follows, whose houses are Gemini and Virgo. Gemini is diurnal, and Virgo is nocturnal. Because Mercury is of a common nature, these are not conjoined with the houses of the Sun and Moon by any aspect.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Buying Property
text: SI QUIS quaestionem de emenda domo proponat, utrum eam obtinere possit necne. Querenti Ascendentem, eius dominum, et planetam a quo separatur Luna tribuas: agro quartam, dominum eius, et Lunam: pretio autem decimam cum suo domino. Quod si dicere nolit quis sit ipsius agri venditor: venditori septimam, eius dominum et planetam cui Luna applicat, dabis. Si tamen venditoris nomen dicere vellet: alius significator ei dandus esset: ut si fratris agrum emere optaret: tertiam ac eius dominum venditori daremus. Attamen quoniam venditoris nomen (vel eo quod ignorat, aut alia de causa) dicere non placet: ei ut dictum est, septima cum suo domino, et planetam cui Luna applicat, dabitur. Jam vero ut quid de re futurum sit agnoscamus: videndum est quaenam sit omnium illorum significatorum dispositio. Si enim dominus Ascendentis et dominus septimae simul juncti sint, vel inter eos applicatio quaedam fuerit, aut luminis translatio, rem facile obtinebit, praecipue si receptio adsit, & applicatio aut aspectus amicitiae fuerit. Quod si haec non sint: videndum est an dominus septimae sit in Ascendente, vel Ascendentis dominus in septima: id enim perfectionis rei author est. Pariter judicandum est, si conjunctio quaedam aut applicatio aut luminis translatio, inter planetam a quo separatur Luna, et eum cui applicat, reperta sit: modo per Lunam translatio non fiat. Aut etiam si ille a quo separatur Luna in septima, vel ille cui applicat in prima collocatus sit: querens rem quaesitam habebit. Si autem nihil omnium horum inveniatur: animadvertendum est, an Ascendentis dominus et dominus quartae et Luna simul juncti sint, aut inter eos applicatio quaedam, aut luminis translatio fuerit: vel si dominus quartae aut Luna in Ascendente reperiantur, aut Ascendentis dominus in quarta: haec enim aliorum vice rem absolvunt. Eodem modo judicandum est, de domini decimae domus dispositione cum Ascendentis.
translation: If someone proposes a question about buying a house, whether he can obtain it or not. To the querent, you attribute the Ascendant, its lord, and the planet from which the Moon separates; to the property, the fourth house, its lord, and the Moon; to the price, the tenth house with its lord. If he does not wish to say who the seller of the land is: to the seller, you will give the seventh house, its lord, and the planet to which the Moon applies. If, however, he wished to say the seller's name: another significator would be given to him: for example, if he wished to buy his brother's land, we would give the third house and its lord to the seller. But since he does not wish to say the seller's name (either because he does not know it, or for another reason): as said, the seventh house with its lord, and the planet to which the Moon applies, will be given to him. Now, in order that we may know what will happen concerning the matter: it must be seen what the disposition of all those significators is. For if the lord of the Ascendant and the lord of the seventh are joined together, or there is some application or translation of light between them, he will easily obtain the matter, especially if reception is present, and the application or aspect is one of friendship. If these are not present: it must be seen whether the lord of the seventh is in the Ascendant, or the lord of the Ascendant in the seventh: for this is the author of the perfection of the matter. Likewise, it is to be judged if some conjunction or application or translation of light is found between the planet from which the Moon separates and the one to which it applies: provided that the translation does not happen through the Moon. Or also if the one from whom the Moon separates is in the seventh, or the one to whom it applies is placed in the first: the querent will have the matter sought. If, however, none of these are found: it must be observed whether the lord of the Ascendant and the lord of the fourth and the Moon are joined together, or if there is some application or translation of light between them: or if the lord of the fourth or the Moon are found in the Ascendant, or the lord of the Ascendant in the fourth: for these, in place of the others, perfect the matter. In the same way, judgment is to be made concerning the disposition of the lord of the tenth house with the Ascendant's.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Determining Ascendant Lord
text: Sed quoniam Sol in decimae domus principio inventus est, ascendentem quadrato aspectu aspiciens, & Mars in domo sexta nullam cum ascendente communionem habens, Sol ascendentis dominus erit. Mars tamen cum Sole particeps est, ob eius plurimas in Ariete dignitates, & in ascendente cum Sole dominium obtinet: quamvis Sol principatum teneat. Hac igitur ratione reliquarum domorum domini inquirendi sunt.
translation: But since the Sun is found at the beginning of the tenth house, aspecting the Ascendant with a square aspect, and Mars in the sixth house having no communion with the Ascendant, the Sun will be the lord of the Ascendant. Mars, however, is a participant with the Sun, because of its many dignities in Aries, and obtains rulership in the Ascendant with the Sun, although the Sun holds the principality. By this method, therefore, the lords of the remaining houses are to be inquired.

author: Claude Dariot
title: Woman's Pregnancy and Fetus Gender
text: VONIAM mulieres aliquádo se vtero gerere aiunt, aut negat, vel ignorant sciréque cupiunt: cuius rei gratia saepissime nobis adfertur quaestio. Quo ut breui ter soluamus, animaduertendus est habitus domini quintae domus, domini ascendentis & Lunae adinui cem, aut cum fortunatis ac angulis. Si igitur dominus ascendentis & Luna in domo quinta sunt, vel quintae dominus in ascendente ab infortunis liber fuerit, mulierem vtero gerere oftendunt. Dispositiones etiam significatoris cum domino ascendentis & Luna, de quibus saepius anteà dictum est, idem denuntiant. Si etiam ascendentis dominus aut significator planetam in angulo existentem aspicit, conceptum denuntiat. Contrâ verò quando planetam ab angu lo cadente aspicit, aut si vtero gerit, abortum patietur: id quod etia signum mobile in ascendente, vel infestus planeta promittit. Signum autem fixum in ascendente, & in codem fortunatus planeta veram conceptionem portendit. Planeta item plures in quinta domo dignitates habens, & in ea situs,&à Luna aspectus, ve ram conceptionem prenuntiat. Fortitudo etiam domini quintae domus in ascendente aut decima idem non minus predicat. Cùm verò mulier vtero gerit, sciréque desideras vtrum masculum, an verò foemellam pariet: ascendentis vide an dominus ascendentis Luna, & dominus quin tae domus, in signo masculino sedem habeat, vel plu res eorum:quoniam mulierem vtero marem gerere denuntiant. Si autem signa foeminina tenuerint, foe mellam arguunt. Aut simili modo vide an dominus horae & dominus domus filiorum & Luna, aut ex his duo, in masculinis signis fuerint, quia marem adducunt:in foemininis verò foeminam. Aliquam domus sexte quaestionem proposuissem eámq; explicauissem, nisi tertius nostri operis tractatus ad hac propemodum totus referretur. Quare de hac dictum satis, quaestionem vnicam domus septimae aggrediar.
translation: Since women sometimes say they are pregnant, or deny it, or are ignorant and wish to know: for which reason a question is very often brought to us. So that we may briefly resolve it, the condition of the lord of the fifth house, the lord of the ascendant, and the Moon, together, or with benefics and angles, must be observed. If, therefore, the lord of the ascendant and the Moon are in the fifth house, or the lord of the fifth is in the ascendant and free from malefics, they show that the woman is pregnant. The dispositions of the significator with the lord of the ascendant and the Moon, about which much has been said before, also announce the same. If also the lord of the ascendant or the significator aspects a planet existing in an angle, it announces conception. Conversely, when it aspects a planet falling from an angle, or if she is pregnant, she will suffer an abortion: which a mutable sign in the ascendant, or an afflicted planet, also promises. A fixed sign in the ascendant, however, and a benefic planet in the same, portends a true conception. Likewise, a planet having many dignities in the fifth house, and situated therein, and aspected by the Moon, foretells a true conception. The strength of the lord of the fifth house in the ascendant or tenth also predicts the same no less. But when a woman is pregnant, and you desire to know whether she will bear a male or a female: see if the lord of the ascendant, the Moon, and the lord of the fifth house have their seat in a masculine sign, or if several of them do: because they announce that the woman will bear a male. If, however, they hold feminine signs, they indicate a female. Or in a similar way, see if the lord of the hour and the lord of the house of children and the Moon, or two of these, are in masculine signs, because they bring a male: but in feminine signs, a female. I would have proposed and explained some question of the sixth house, had not the third treatise of our work almost entirely referred to this. Therefore, enough has been said about this, I will approach a single question of the seventh house. (I would have proposed and explained some question of the sixth house, had not the third treatise of our work almost entirely referred to this. Therefore, enough has been said about this, I will approach a single question of the seventh house.) author: Claude Dariot
title: Will one obtain the desired wife?
text: Ex hac quaestione à viro proposita eam etiam quam mulier adferet soluere fas erit: vnicuique tamen significatorem proprium tribuendo: soluitur autem haec hac ratione. Querenti in primis ascendentem,domi num ascendentis, Lunam Planetam à quo separatur Luna, & Solem significatores tribue. Mulieribus verò septimam, eius dominum planetam cui Luna applicat, & Venerem. Deinde animaduerte qualis sit dispositio domini ascendentis & Lunae cum septimae domus domino:& planetae à quo Luna separatur, cum eo cui applicat, ac solis cum Venere. Applicatio enim sextilis vel trini effectum facilem significat:quadrati verò & oppositionis difficilem:separatio autem dimi nutionem & abolitionem arguit. Si itaque ascendentis dominus aut Luna septimam domum teneat, aut planeta à quo separatur Luna, planetam cui Luna applicat, aspiciat aut ei applicat, aut Sol Veneri,ma trimonium futurum (cum labore tamen & dificulta te) arguunt:idque maximè si applicatio inimicitiae fuerit. Haec enim inimicitias & odium pariunt,atque ob id opus destruunt, nisi meliores aliae significationes interueniant. Cùm verò septimae dominus in prima domo est collocatus, aut cum ascendentis do mino coniunctus, aut cui Luna applicat planeta, eum à quo ipsa separatur trino aut sextili aspectu respicit, aut eodem aspectu ei applicat,aut Venus Soli:muliere facilimè quaerens invxorem ducet. Aegre tamen id fiet si nulla adsit receptio & replicatio quadrati aut oppo- sitionis fuerit. Dominus etiam ascendentis receptus, actione significat. Quod si aut nihil praedictorum inue neris, animaduerte an sit quaedam inter eos luminis translatio, aut collatio: quoniam hec non minus quàm praedicta, querenti vxorem quam ducere cupit, promittunt:illud tamen interuenientibus legatis perficietur. Luna denique ab infortunijs libera &recepta idem promittit.
translation: From this question proposed by a man, it will also be right to resolve that which a woman brings: assigning to each their proper significator: it is resolved in this manner. To the querent, first assign the ascendant, the lord of the ascendant, the Moon, the planet from which the Moon separates, and the Sun as significators. To the woman, however, the seventh house, its lord, the planet to which the Moon applies, and Venus. Then observe what kind of disposition exists between the lord of the ascendant and the Moon with the lord of the seventh house; and between the planet from which the Moon separates, with the one to which it applies, and the Sun with Venus. For a sextile or trine application signifies an easy effect; but a square and opposition, a difficult one; separation, however, argues for diminution and abolition. If, therefore, the lord of the ascendant or the Moon holds the seventh house, or the planet from which the Moon separates aspects or applies to the planet to which the Moon applies, or the Sun to Venus, they argue that marriage will happen (though with labor and difficulty): and this especially if the application was one of enmity. For these produce enmities and hatred, and on account of this, they destroy the work, unless better other significations intervene. But when the lord of the seventh is placed in the first house, or conjoined with the lord of the ascendant, or the planet to which the Moon applies aspects with a trine or sextile aspect the one from which she separates, or applies to it with the same aspect, or Venus to the Sun: the querent will most easily marry the woman. However, this will happen with difficulty if there is no reception and a replication of a square or opposition. The lord of the ascendant, also, if received, signifies action. But if you find none of the aforesaid, observe whether there is some translation of light or collection between them: because these, no less than the aforesaid, promise the querent the wife he desires to marry: but that will be accomplished through intervening messengers. Finally, the Moon free from malefics and received promises the same.
